title: Microthelys santa-elenae
intro: Microthelys santa-elenae, also known as Santa Elena orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and treating various infections. Microthelys santa-elenae contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and has been traditionally used in the form of a tea or a topical ointment to support its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Microthelys santa-elenae is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to the Santa Elena Island in the Galapagos Islands. This species is a part of the genus Microthelys, which is a group of epiphytic orchids. The classification of Microthelys santa-elenae is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta.
